disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Inflammatory back pain pattern — prolonged morning stiffness (several hours), pain eases with movement and worsens with rest/immobility
trigger_question_examples: How long does the stiffness last in the mornings?
Does the pain get better or worse when you move around versus when you rest?
Is it worse in the morning or after you've been lying still for a while?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The stiffness this morning went on for hours — nearly the whole morning before it eased off a bit. And oddly, lying still makes it worse; gentle moving loosens it slightly.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: It's just really painful all the time, I haven't thought about that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cauda equina screen NEGATIVE — no numbness in the groin/saddle area; bladder and bowel control normal
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any numbness around your groin, buttocks, or between your legs (the saddle area)?
Any change in your ability to control your bladder or bowels — any leaking or not being able to go?
Is there any numbness when you wipe or in the area you'd sit on a saddle?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — no numbness down there, and my bladder and bowels are working completely normally.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't think so, it's really just the back pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Leg weakness is pain-limited only — no true motor weakness, no numbness or pins-and-needles down the legs; normal sensation in both legs
trigger_question_examples: When you say you can't walk — is it because of the pain, or do your legs actually feel weak or give way on their own?
Any numbness, tingling, or pins-and-needles running down either leg?
Can you feel your legs and feet normally when you touch them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's the pain stopping me — my legs feel strong enough, they don't give way on their own. No numbness or tingling down my legs, and I can feel them fine when I touch them.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I just can't move because it hurts too much.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known longstanding inflammatory joint condition with prior similar flares that settled with anti-inflammatories and specialist care
trigger_question_examples: Have you had episodes like this before?
Can you tell me about your joint condition and how it's usually managed?
Has your specialist treated flare-ups like this in the past?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I get flare-ups of my joint condition every so often — they usually settle down with the anti-inflammatories and my specialist keeps an eye on it. This one's just far worse than usual.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Already taken maximal doses of paracetamol AND ibuprofen with no relief — simple analgesia has failed
trigger_question_examples: What have you already taken for the pain, and did any of it help?
Have you tried any anti-inflammatory or pain medication today?
How much paracetamol or ibuprofen have you had, and over what period?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've taken the maximum paracetamol and the maximum ibuprofen for the day already and honestly neither has touched it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone with high fall risk — cannot transfer safely, floor rugs and low lighting on path to bathroom
trigger_question_examples: Is there anyone at home with you or who can help you right now?
How are you managing to get around the house safely?
Do you have any support if you needed help getting up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I live on my own and there's no one here. I'm really worried about falling — there are rugs and it's dim between the bed and the bathroom and I can barely move.
